task_completion.description: Open the facility chemical inventory file
task_completion.visual_evidence: A spreadsheet application or text editor displaying the contents of 'facility_chemical_inventory.csv', showing a list of chemicals and their locations.
A web browser window open to the CAMEO Chemicals website (cameochemicals.noaa.gov).

task_completion.description: Use CAMEO Chemicals to check reactivity between inventory chemicals
task_completion.visual_evidence: The CAMEO Chemicals interface showing a list of added chemicals (MyChemicals) from the inventory, or a reactivity prediction table/worksheet involving these chemicals.

task_completion.description: Create the storage audit report file
task_completion.visual_evidence: A text editor window open with a file named 'storage_audit_report.txt' in the ~/Documents directory.

task_completion.description: Identify dangerous chemical pairs and their locations in the report
task_completion.visual_evidence: The text in the report explicitly names dangerous chemical pairs (e.g., Hydrogen Peroxide and Acetone, Sulfuric Acid and Sodium Cyanide) and includes their specific locations (e.g., Building A Bay 7, Building B Bay 2).

task_completion.description: Include hazard descriptions, severity ratings, standards violations, and corrective actions in the report
task_completion.visual_evidence: The text in the report contains descriptions of the hazardous reactions (e.g., HCN gas, explosive peroxides), severity ratings (critical/high/medium), mentions of NFPA/OSHA standards, and recommended corrective actions for the identified pairs.

integrity.description: The agent used a web browser to interact with CAMEO Chemicals
integrity.visual_evidence: A standard web browser (like Chrome or Firefox) is visible navigating the CAMEO website, rather than terminal commands like curl or python scripts.

integrity.description: The agent used a text editor GUI to write the report
integrity.visual_evidence: A text editor GUI (like gedit, nano, or VS Code) is visible with the report content, rather than the file being generated entirely via terminal commands (e.g., echo or cat) without visual inspection.

integrity.description: The report contains detailed chemical hazard information sourced from CAMEO Chemicals
integrity.visual_evidence: The report includes specific chemical reaction details and hazards that go beyond the basic task description, indicating genuine use of the CAMEO tool rather than just copying the prompt.

privileged_info_for_vlm: The inventory contains 15 chemicals across 4 buildings. Key dangerous pairs to look for in the report: 1) Sulfuric Acid (Bldg B Bay 2) + Sodium Cyanide (Bldg C Bay 1) which produces highly toxic HCN gas. 2) Hydrogen Peroxide >60% (Bldg A Bay 7) + Acetone (Bldg A Bay 5) which forms explosive peroxides. Other potential pairs include Chlorine + Ammonia, Nitric Acid + Organics, and Sodium Azide + Metals/Acids. The report must be located at ~/Documents/storage_audit_report.txt and contain specific pairs, locations, hazard descriptions, severity ratings, NFPA/OSHA violations, and corrective actions.
